paragraphs.text:
%md
## Why Agents Beat Zero-Shot Prompts at Big Star Collectibles

### The Business Problem

Big Star Collectibles' inventory specialists have been using AI chatbots for months, and the reviews are... mixed.

*"I asked it about a client's item status and it told me how to log into our system. I know how to log in! I wanted the actual status."*

*"It gave me great advice on updating item records, but it can't actually update them. I still have to do everything manually."*

The chatbots are helpful for general questions, but they can't access Big Star Collectibles' data or take action. They explain processes without executing them.

### What You'll Learn

In this lab, you'll directly compare three approaches to understand why agents are transforming how work gets done:

| Approach | Can Access Data | Can Modify Data | Can Coordinate |
|----------|-----------------|-----------------|----------------|
| **SELECT AI CHAT** (zero-shot) | No | No | No |
| **SELECT AI** | Yes | No | No |
| **SELECT AI AGENT** | Yes | Yes | Yes |

You'll ask the same question about item submissions to each approach and see the dramatic difference.

**What you'll build:** A comparison showing exactly when to use each approach.

**Estimated Time:** 10 minutes

msg.data:
Why Agents Beat Zero-Shot Prompts at Big Star Collectibles

The Business Problem

Big Star Collectibles' inventory specialists have been using AI chatbots for months, and the reviews are... mixed.

"I asked it about a client's item status and it told me how to log into our system. I know how to log in! I wanted the actual status."

"It gave me great advice on updating item records, but it can't actually update them. I still have to do everything manually."

The chatbots are helpful for general questions, but they can't access Big Star Collectibles' data or take action. They explain processes without executing them.

What You'll Learn

In this lab, you'll directly compare three approaches to understand why agents are transforming how work gets done:

ApproachCan Access DataCan Modify DataCan Coordinate
SELECT AI CHAT (zero-shot)NoNoNo
SELECT AIYesNoNo
SELECT AI AGENTYesYesYes

You'll ask the same question about item submissions to each approach and see the dramatic difference.

What you'll build: A comparison showing exactly when to use each approach.

Estimated Time: 10 minutes

paragraphs.text:
%md
## Set Your AI Profile

First, activate your AI profile for the zero-shot and SELECT AI comparisons.

msg.data:
Set Your AI Profile

First, activate your AI profile for the zero-shot and SELECT AI comparisons.

paragraphs.text:
%script
EXEC DBMS_CLOUD_AI.SET_PROFILE('genai');

%md
## Try Zero-Shot: Ask a Procedural Question

Zero-shot (SELECT AI CHAT) goes directly to the LLM for general knowledge. Watch what happens when you ask how to process an item submission.

**What you'll see:** A helpful explanation of the steps, but you still have to do each step yourself. The AI advises; it doesn't act.

msg.data:
Try Zero-Shot: Ask a Procedural Question

Zero-shot (SELECT AI CHAT) goes directly to the LLM for general knowledge. Watch what happens when you ask how to process an item submission.

What you'll see: A helpful explanation of the steps, but you still have to do each step yourself. The AI advises; it doesn't act.

paragraphs.text:
%script
SELECT AI CHAT How do I process an item submission at Big Star Collectibles;

%md
## Try Zero-Shot: Ask About YOUR Data

Now try asking about a specific item submission in your system.

**What you'll see:** The AI cannot answer this because it has no access to your data. It gives generic advice about how to check item status, but it doesn't know anything about YOUR submission ITEM-20260115-1042.

This is the fundamental limitation of zero-shot: great for general knowledge, useless for your specific business data.

msg.data:
Try Zero-Shot: Ask About YOUR Data

Now try asking about a specific item submission in your system.

What you'll see: The AI cannot answer this because it has no access to your data. It gives generic advice about how to check item status, but it doesn't know anything about YOUR submission ITEM-20260115-1042.

This is the fundamental limitation of zero-shot: great for general knowledge, useless for your specific business data.

paragraphs.text:
%script
SELECT AI CHAT What is the status of item submission ITEM-20260115-1042 at Big Star Collectibles;

%md
## Try Zero-Shot: Ask It to DO Something

What happens when you ask the AI to take action on an item submission?

**What you'll see:** The AI explains HOW to update an item status but cannot actually do it. Zero-shot can advise; it cannot act.

msg.data:
Try Zero-Shot: Ask It to DO Something

What happens when you ask the AI to take action on an item submission?

What you'll see: The AI explains HOW to update an item status but cannot actually do it. Zero-shot can advise; it cannot act.

paragraphs.text:
%script
SELECT AI CHAT Update item submission ITEM-20260115-1042 to listed;

%md
## Create Sample Data for SELECT AI

Before we try SELECT AI (without CHAT or AGENT), we need actual data. Let's create an item submissions table.

Notice the detailed comments. These help Select AI understand what each column contains.

msg.data:
Create Sample Data for SELECT AI

Before we try SELECT AI (without CHAT or AGENT), we need actual data. Let's create an item submissions table.

Notice the detailed comments. These help Select AI understand what each column contains.

paragraphs.text:
%md
## Add the Table to Your AI Profile

Select AI needs to know which tables it can access. Add your new table to the profile's object list.

msg.data:
Add the Table to Your AI Profile

Select AI needs to know which tables it can access. Add your new table to the profile's object list.

paragraphs.text:
%md
## Try SELECT AI: Query Your Data

Now use SELECT AI NARRATE to ask about item submission ITEM-20260115-1042. This time, SELECT AI CAN read your data.

**What you'll see:** The actual status "AUTHENTICATING" from your database. Compare this to zero-shot which could only give generic advice.

msg.data:
Try SELECT AI: Query Your Data

Now use SELECT AI NARRATE to ask about item submission ITEM-20260115-1042. This time, SELECT AI CAN read your data.

What you'll see: The actual status "AUTHENTICATING" from your database. Compare this to zero-shot which could only give generic advice.

paragraphs.text:
%script
SELECT AI NARRATE What is the status of item submission ITEM-20260115-1042;

%md
## Try SELECT AI: Ask It to Update

Now try to update the item submission status using SELECT AI.

**What you'll see:** SELECT AI cannot update data. It can only read. It might generate a SELECT statement or explain what you'd need to do, but it won't actually change anything.

msg.data:
Try SELECT AI: Ask It to Update

Now try to update the item submission status using SELECT AI.

What you'll see: SELECT AI cannot update data. It can only read. It might generate a SELECT statement or explain what you'd need to do, but it won't actually change anything.

paragraphs.text:
%script
SELECT AI NARRATE Update item submission ITEM-20260115-1042 to listed;

%md
## Verify No Update Happened

Let's confirm the item submission was NOT updated.

**What you'll see:** The status is still AUTHENTICATING. SELECT AI can read but cannot write.

msg.data:
Verify No Update Happened

Let's confirm the item submission was NOT updated.

What you'll see: The status is still AUTHENTICATING. SELECT AI can read but cannot write.

paragraphs.text:
%script
SELECT submission_id, item_status FROM sample_items WHERE submission_id = 'ITEM-20260115-1042';

%md
## Create an Agent That Can Act

Now let's build an agent that can both READ and WRITE. We'll give it two tools: one to look up items and one to update them.

In this lab, we're using **function-based tools** instead of SQL tools. A function-based tool wraps a PL/SQL function you write, giving you complete control over what the tool does. This is how you give agents the ability to make changes to your data, not just read it.

msg.data:
Create an Agent That Can Act

Now let's build an agent that can both READ and WRITE. We'll give it two tools: one to look up items and one to update them.

In this lab, we're using function-based tools instead of SQL tools. A function-based tool wraps a PL/SQL function you write, giving you complete control over what the tool does. This is how you give agents the ability to make changes to your data, not just read it.

paragraphs.text:
%md
## Create the Lookup Function

This function takes an item ID and returns the item details. It's a simple read operation that the agent will use to check on items before taking action.

msg.data:
Create the Lookup Function

This function takes an item ID and returns the item details. It's a simple read operation that the agent will use to check on items before taking action.

paragraphs.text:
%md
## Create the Update Function

This function actually changes data in the database. Notice it first checks the current status, then makes the update. The agent will use this to approve or deny items.

The `PRAGMA AUTONOMOUS_TRANSACTION` allows it to commit changes independently.

msg.data:
Create the Update Function

This function actually changes data in the database. Notice it first checks the current status, then makes the update. The agent will use this to approve or deny items.

The PRAGMA AUTONOMOUS_TRANSACTION allows it to commit changes independently.

paragraphs.text:
%md
## Register the Tools

Now we turn these functions into tools the agent can use. The `instruction` tells the agent when and how to use each tool. Notice how we tell the agent to check the item status before updating—this is how you build smart behavior into your agent.

msg.data:
Register the Tools

Now we turn these functions into tools the agent can use. The instruction tells the agent when and how to use each tool. Notice how we tell the agent to check the item status before updating—this is how you build smart behavior into your agent.

paragraphs.text:
%md
## Create the Agent, Task, and Team

Now we put it all together. The agent gets a role that tells it how to behave. The task gives it specific instructions and access to both tools. The team makes it all runnable.

Notice how we give the agent access to TWO tools—it can decide which one to use based on what you ask.

msg.data:
Create the Agent, Task, and Team

Now we put it all together. The agent gets a role that tells it how to behave. The task gives it specific instructions and access to both tools. The team makes it all runnable.

Notice how we give the agent access to TWO tools—it can decide which one to use based on what you ask.

paragraphs.text:
%md
## Check Current Item Submission Status

Before testing the agent, verify the current status of item submission ITEM-20260115-1042.

**What you'll see:** Status is AUTHENTICATING.

msg.data:
Check Current Item Submission Status

Before testing the agent, verify the current status of item submission ITEM-20260115-1042.

What you'll see: Status is AUTHENTICATING.

paragraphs.text:
%script
SELECT submission_id, collector, item_status FROM sample_items WHERE submission_id = 'ITEM-20260115-1042';

%md
## Ask the Agent to Check and Update

Now ask the agent to check the item submission and list it if it's authenticating. This requires the agent to:
1. Look up the current status
2. Make a decision based on what it finds
3. Take action (update to listed)

**What you'll see:** The agent checks the status (AUTHENTICATING), decides it should be updated, and changes it to LISTED.

msg.data:
Ask the Agent to Check and Update

Now ask the agent to check the item submission and list it if it's authenticating. This requires the agent to:

  1. Look up the current status
  2. Make a decision based on what it finds
  3. Take action (update to listed)

What you'll see: The agent checks the status (AUTHENTICATING), decides it should be updated, and changes it to LISTED.

paragraphs.text:
%md
## Verify the Agent Made the Change

Let's confirm the update actually happened.

**What you'll see:** Status is now LISTED. The agent didn't just talk about updating. It actually did it.

msg.data:
Verify the Agent Made the Change

Let's confirm the update actually happened.

What you'll see: Status is now LISTED. The agent didn't just talk about updating. It actually did it.

paragraphs.text:
%script
SELECT submission_id, collector, item_status FROM sample_items WHERE submission_id = 'ITEM-20260115-1042';

%md
## Test Conditional Logic

Now try the same request on item submission ITEM-20260114-0821, which is SUBMITTED (not authenticating).

**What you'll see:** The agent looks up the submission, sees it's SUBMITTED, and correctly decides NOT to update it because the condition ("if it's authenticating") isn't met.

This is intelligent coordination. The agent makes decisions based on data.

msg.data:
Test Conditional Logic

Now try the same request on item submission ITEM-20260114-0821, which is SUBMITTED (not authenticating).

What you'll see: The agent looks up the submission, sees it's SUBMITTED, and correctly decides NOT to update it because the condition ("if it's authenticating") isn't met.

This is intelligent coordination. The agent makes decisions based on data.

paragraphs.text:
%script
SELECT AI AGENT Check item submission ITEM-20260114-0821 and if it is authenticating, mark it as listed;

%md
## Review the Tool Execution History

Every tool call is logged. You can see exactly what the agent did.

**What you'll see:** The sequence of tool calls: lookups followed by updates (or just lookups when no update was needed).

msg.data:
Review the Tool Execution History

Every tool call is logged. You can see exactly what the agent did.

What you'll see: The sequence of tool calls: lookups followed by updates (or just lookups when no update was needed).

paragraphs.text:
%script
SELECT 
    tool_name,
    TO_CHAR(start_date, 'HH24:MI:SS') as called_at,
    SUBSTR(output, 1, 80) as result
FROM USER_AI_AGENT_TOOL_HISTORY
ORDER BY start_date DESC
FETCH FIRST 10 ROWS ONLY;

%md
## The Comparison Summary

| Approach | Can Access Your Data | Can Modify Data | Can Coordinate Steps |
|----------|---------------------|-----------------|----------------------|
| SELECT AI CHAT | No | No | No |
| SELECT AI | Yes | No | No |
| SELECT AI AGENT | Yes | Yes | Yes |

**Use SELECT AI CHAT when:**
- You need general knowledge answers
- No data access required
- You want advice or explanation

**Use SELECT AI when:**
- You need to query your data with natural language
- Read-only access is sufficient

**Use SELECT AI AGENT when:**
- You need to read AND write data
- Decisions depend on data (conditional logic)
- Actions need coordination across multiple steps

msg.data:
The Comparison Summary

ApproachCan Access Your DataCan Modify DataCan Coordinate Steps
SELECT AI CHATNoNoNo
SELECT AIYesNoNo
SELECT AI AGENTYesYesYes

Use SELECT AI CHAT when:

  • You need general knowledge answers
  • No data access required
  • You want advice or explanation

Use SELECT AI when:

  • You need to query your data with natural language
  • Read-only access is sufficient

Use SELECT AI AGENT when:

  • You need to read AND write data
  • Decisions depend on data (conditional logic)
  • Actions need coordination across multiple steps

paragraphs.text:
%md
## What You Learned

You directly compared three approaches:

- **SELECT AI CHAT**: Cannot access your data; can only give generic advice
- **SELECT AI**: Can read your data but cannot modify it  
- **SELECT AI AGENT**: Can read your data, make decisions, and take action

You watched the agent coordinate: check status → decide → act → report. And you verified the data actually changed.

**Key takeaway:** The difference is not just intelligence. It is action. Zero-shot AI tells you what to do. SELECT AI can read. Agents do the work.

msg.data:
What You Learned

You directly compared three approaches:

  • SELECT AI CHAT: Cannot access your data; can only give generic advice
  • SELECT AI: Can read your data but cannot modify it
  • SELECT AI AGENT: Can read your data, make decisions, and take action

You watched the agent coordinate: check status → decide → act → report. And you verified the data actually changed.

Key takeaway: The difference is not just intelligence. It is action. Zero-shot AI tells you what to do. SELECT AI can read. Agents do the work.
